# WEPPY — Canonical Web Docs

> WEPPY is an MCP server that lets AI coding agents control a live Roblox Studio session and interact with scripts, instances, terrain, lighting, assets, audio, animations, and UI Studio workflows.

The web dashboard includes a Connection topology for multiple AI agents, the MCP server, and multiple connected Studio Targets, with copyable Studio IDs and routing status for multi-Studio workflows.

This package intentionally does not ship a standalone full documentation surface.

Canonical documentation lives on the web:

- Install and setup: https://weppyai.com/en/install
- Product documentation: https://weppyai.com/en/docs
- Pro plans: https://weppyai.com/plans/

For installation, start from the web install page and follow its recommended one-line installer first. If terminal or PowerShell is uncomfortable, use the terminal-free web installer on that same page. If automated install fails, use the relevant app-specific manual guide.

Optional WEPPY Roblox AI Toolkit:

The shared plugin id is `weppy-roblox-ai-toolkit`. Claude Code, Codex, and Antigravity use the same Roblox workflow skill payload.

- Claude Code:

```bash
claude plugin marketplace add hope1026/weppy-roblox-mcp --scope user
claude plugin install weppy-roblox-ai-toolkit@hope1026-roblox-mcp --scope user
```

- Codex:

```bash
codex plugin marketplace add hope1026/weppy-roblox-mcp
```

After adding the Codex marketplace, restart Codex, open Plugin Directory, and install WEPPY Roblox AI Toolkit.

- Antigravity:

The installer uses `~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json` as the shared global MCP config. In a CLI-only profile, verified native plugin MCP replaces the shared direct WEPPY entry. When Antigravity IDE and CLI coexist, both plugin views stay skill-only and one shared direct `weppy-roblox-mcp` definition serves both. Outside the verified OS and version matrix, or when native discovery fails, the installer keeps that shared definition as the MCP fallback and asks the user to restart and verify. Other MCP servers are preserved. Do not pass a GitHub URL directly to `agy plugin install`.
